back_translation: And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: a man or woman, when he makes a special vow, a vow of a Nazirite, to separate himself to the LORD,

from wine and strong drink he shall separate himself; vinegar of wine and vinegar of strong drink he shall not drink; and all liquid of grapes he shall not drink; and fresh or dried grapes he shall not eat.
All the days of his separation, from anything that is made from the vine of wine, from seeds even to skin, he shall not eat.

All the days of the vow of his separation, a razor shall not pass over his head; until the days he separates himself to the LORD are completed, he shall be holy; he shall let the hair of his head grow long.
All the days of his separation to the LORD, he shall not go near a dead person.
For his father or for his mother, for his brother or for his sister, he shall not defile himself for them at their death, because the consecration of his God is upon his head.
All the days of his separation, holy is he to the LORD.
And if someone dies beside him suddenly, in an instant, and defiles his head of separation, then he shall shave his head on the day of his cleansing — on the seventh day he shall shave it.
And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two sons of a pigeon to the priest, to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

And the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and one as a burnt offering, and shall make atonement for him from what he sinned regarding the dead body; and he shall consecrate his head on that day.

And he shall separate himself to the LORD the days of his separation, and shall bring a lamb a year old as a guilt offering; and the first days shall fall (not be counted), because his separation was defiled.
And this is the law of the Nazirite, on the day of completing the days of his separation, he shall bring himself to the entrance of the Tent of Meeting.

And he shall present his offering to the LORD: one male lamb a year old without blemish as a burnt offering, and one female lamb a year old without blemish as a sin offering, and one ram without blemish as a peace offering,
and a basket of unleavened bread, fine flour cakes mixed with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and their grain offerings and their drink offerings.
And the priest shall present them before the LORD, and shall make his sin offering and his burnt offering.
And the ram he shall offer as a sacrifice of peace offering to the LORD, with the basket of unleavened bread; and the priest shall offer his grain offering and his drink offering.

And the Nazirite shall shave, at the entrance of the Tent of Meeting, the head of his separation; and he shall take the hair of the head of his separation and put it on the fire which is under the sacrifice of peace offering.

And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder from the ram, and one cake of unleavened bread from the basket, and one wafer of unleavened bread, and shall put them on the palms of the Nazirite after he has shaved off his separation.

And the priest shall wave them, a wave offering, before the LORD; it is holy for the priest, with the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution; and after that the Nazirite may drink wine.

This is the law of the Nazirite who vows, his offering to the LORD for his separation, besides what his hand can afford; according to the word of his vow which he vowed, so he shall do, according to the law of his separation."
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to Aaron and to his sons, saying: Thus you shall bless the sons of Israel; say to them:
May the LORD bless you and keep you.
May the LORD cause His face to shine upon you and be gracious to you.
May the LORD lift up His face upon you and set peace for you.
And they shall put My name upon the sons of Israel, and I will bless them."
